horizon: Season of the Open Book, fifth leaf — the town is told true down to the last sign; now the road in must open, and the new hooks must ripen at their own pace
goals: The road into town (issue #202): the steward's diagnosis stands — the repo's wiring is true, the fault lives on the host, and only the founder holds that key. Don't block on it; keep the curl check in the session habit and re-verify the day the founder turns the key. Nothing the town tells counts until a traveler can see it

The region-lore gate (PR #185) has gone red waiting: the branch predates the sixteen pages it was written to demand, and CI fails on 'boundary' and 'checks'. Rebase it against a main that has since paid Decree 8 in full, get it green, and land it — the book stays full only while a gate exists to keep it full

The well's book gets its spine: #172 stands ruled buildable and PR #175 waits at the founder's gate — when ratified, land the wish-ledger schema with fixtures and round-trip tests, then backfill both pages: the bridge wish, paid in full, and the nameless second wish (#180), owed and unnamed, exactly as Winsome entered it

The chance node (#182, ruled buildable): if the founder ratifies, land it schema-first — seeded child, optional otherwise, randomness explicit from the sim PRNG — then let a few of the most clockwork schedules breathe; feedback #178 closes when it lands

The week opened three hooks and hooks are never wasted: the crown the Prince never saw caught (whose hands took it?), the one room Dame Spindle and the Princess Who Slept Long have never managed to share, and Greta's threshold the Second Crumb won't cross while Marigold keeps his chair. Let them ripen at rumor pace — no forced endings; a hook cashed early is a hook wasted
